meaning: 璜加雷[新西兰北岛北岸港市]
examples: Some whangarei families will have to cough up extra money to send their children to the school of their choice.
The 300,000 project by the Whangarei District Council included major stormwater and drainage improvements to prevent water entering shops and customers slipping in the wet.
Roger Daniel, 37, offered the novel excuse after he was nabbed traveling at over 120 kilometers per hour in a 50 kph zone in the northern town of Whangarei, the DominionPost newspaper reported.
He damaged vertebrae in his neck when his Jeep Cherokee rolled over on a rural road as he was driving from a music festival near Whangarei.
